citeinfo.origin: U.S. Geological Survey, National Geospatial Program
citeinfo.title: Geographic Names Information System (GNIS) Domestic Names AllStates (published 20260513) pipes
citeinfo.geoform: Tabular digital data (for the text files) and vector digital data (for the geodatabase)

descript.abstract: The Geographic Names Information System (GNIS) is the Federal standard for geographic nomenclature. The U.S. Geological Survey developed the GNIS for the U.S. Board on Geographic Names, a Federal inter-agency body chartered by public law to maintain uniform feature name usage throughout the Government and to promulgate standard names to the public. The GNIS is the official repository of domestic geographic names data; the official vehicle for geographic names use by all departments of the Federal Government; and the source for applying geographic names to Federal electronic and printed products of all types.
descript.purpose: The Geographic Names Information System contains information about physical and cultural geographic features of all types in the United States, associated areas, and Antarctica, current and historical, but not including roads and highways. The database holds the Federally recognized name of each feature and defines the feature location by state, county, USGS topographic map, and geographic coordinates. Other attributes include names or spellings other than the official name, feature designations, feature classification, historical and descriptive information, and for some categories the geometric boundaries. The database assigns a unique, permanent feature identifier, the Feature ID, as a standard Federal key for accessing, integrating, or reconciling feature data from multiple data sets. The GNIS provides data to all levels of government and to the public, as well as to numerous applications through a web query site and file download services.
descript.supplinf: The U.S. Board on Geographic Names was created in 1890 and established in its present form by Public Law in 1947 to establish and maintain uniform geographic name usage throughout the Federal Government. The Board serves all government agencies and the public as the central authority to which name inquiries, name issues, and new name proposals can be directed. It is comprised of representatives of Federal agencies associated with land management and cartography. Sharing its responsibilities with the Secretary of the Interior, the Board develops principles, policies, and procedures governing the use of both domestic and foreign geographic names as well as undersea and Antarctic feature names.; {"inventory":"d2b2dad8-12c9-eef3-8cf7-80b85661ec31"}

theme.themekey: UTF-8 Pipe Delimited Text File nan

theme.themekt: The National Map Product Extent Thesaurus
The National Map Product Format Thesaurus

idinfo.useconst: None. Acknowledgment of the U.S. Geological Survey would be appreciated for products derived from these data.

attracc.attraccr: Content is derived from USGS topographic maps during Phase 1 compilation (1976-81), with subsequent entries entered using other Federal, State, and local sources of information.

dataqual.logic: Data is compiled from Federal, State, and local sources and is validated according to the Board on Geographic Names’ Principles, Policies, and Procedures.
dataqual.complete: This dataset contains information about United States and Associated Territories physical and cultural geographic features of all types and classes, with the exception of roads and highways. Subsequent to the original data compilation process, which was taken primarily from Federal maps, the GNIS now relies on input from Federal, State, county, and local government agencies and other approved sources. The completeness, currency, and accuracy of the data varies by area depending on the quality and timeliness of that input.

horizpa.horizpar: Accuracy of these digital data is based upon the use of printed or electronic source graphics which are compiled to meet National Map Accuracy Standards. If maps are not used, data is validated against original source material.

vertacc.vertaccr: Elevation values are obtained using the 3DEP point location service and is dependent on the GNIS primary point location.

citeinfo.title: Records of the U.S. Board on Geographic Names
U.S. Forest Service 1:24,000-scale topographic maps and visitor maps

citeinfo.origin: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration/National Ocean Service
citeinfo.title: National Ocean Service charts

srcinfo.srccitea: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration/National Ocean Service

procstep.procdesc: GNIS Phase I data compilation and edit - Feature name and attribute data were collected from the largest-scale USGS topographic maps, U.S. Forest Service maps, and NOAA Charts available at the time of compilation.
GNIS Phase II data compilation and edit - Feature name and attribute data are collected from official state and local sources and other approved sources including historical maps and documents.

GNIS Maintenance - continuation of Phase II efforts. Federal and state cooperators submit new and edited information as necessary. USGS names data specialists review the submissions and accept or reject based on the information submitted.

cntorgp.cntorg: U.S. Geological Survey, National Geospatial Program

cntinfo.hours: Monday through Friday 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM
cntinfo.cntinst: Metadata information can also be obtained through online services using ScienceBase, https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/ or from The National Map Viewer, at http://nationalmap.usgs.gov

distinfo.distliab: No liability for content or accuracy is presumed by the USBGN or USGS for data.

digtinfo.formcont: fileGDB: Spatial objects with unique identifiers, geographic names data and attributes, and coordinate data Pipe delimited text: geographic names data and attributes, and coordinate data
digtinfo.filedec: ZIP lossless data compression has been applied using a DEFLATE algorithm.
